steps.title: Count novels and protagonists
steps.content: The passage discusses two novels and two protagonists, Catherine Morland and Emily St. Aubert. The phrase must show experiences belonging to the protagonists of the novels.

steps.title: Reject number and possession errors
steps.content: Choice A makes novels possessive but not protagonists. Choice C makes both nouns singular. Choice D uses singular novel even though two novels are being compared.

steps.title: Choose two plural possessives
steps.content: Using plural possessive forms for both novels and protagonists correctly shows the protagonists' experiences in the two novels. The answer is B.
